human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for janitorial-workflow specificity, chemical and exposure-control boundaries, access and QA dependencies, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to janitorial execution, room readiness, supply and chemical controls, QA inspection, deficiency rework, and occupant-impact handling rather than generic facilities support or maintenance dispatch.

Authority sources cover GSA custodial workflow and QA expectations, OSHA chemical and exposure-control guidance, EPA product and remediation boundaries, and a public institutional custodial SLA for service-level terminology and work-request patterns.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, mid-process artifact gaps, dilution and complaint-driven rework, unsupported closure requests, restricted-area blockers, failed QA and mold concerns, trade or remediation boundary rejection, safety-driven reversal, policy conflict escalation, and tenant-specific terminology normalization.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/facilities-services-and-building-operations/janitorial-operations-specialist/ so it remains distinct from project controls, permitting, inspection, safety, superintendent, and licensed-trade lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated facilities-services-and-building-operations janitorial specialist for route execution, janitorial QA, supply and chemical readiness, occupant-impact-aware service handling, and remediation-boundary enforcement.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
